</ix:nonNumeric><p id="xdx_8AC_zFb4A8UK7Kr" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif">The
decrease in the restricted cash account for the year ended December 31, 2024 can be attributed to repairs and maintenance cost.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0">&#160;</p>

</ix:nonNumeric><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" escape="true" id="Fact000545" name="us-gaap:ConcentrationRiskCreditRisk"><p id="xdx_84B_eus-gaap--ConcentrationRiskCreditRisk_zus020HrgEL5" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><b><span id="xdx_86D_zlWR8ii82gnc">Concentration of Credit Risk</span></b></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">The Company
maintains deposits in financial institutions that at times exceed the insured amount of $<span id="xdx_90A_eus-gaap--CashFDICInsuredAmount_iI_c20241231_zpdhyGzZh68j"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:CashFDICInsuredAmount" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31" id="Fact000546" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">250,000</ix:nonFraction>
</span></span><span style="font-size: 10pt">provided by the U.S. Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (&#8220;FDIC&#8221;). The Company
went to an Insured Cash Sweep service (&#8220;ICS&#8221;) in 2021. ICS funds are eligible for multi-million-dollar FDIC insurance that&#8217;s
backed by the full faith and credit of the United States government. Daily cash is swept and deposited to as many banks as needed that
are FDIC insured. This insures no amounts exceed a $<span id="xdx_902_eus-gaap--CashFDICInsuredAmount_iI_c20241231_z4aaY2zhhCK7"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:CashFDICInsuredAmount" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31" id="Fact000547" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">250,000</ix:nonFraction>
</span></span><span style="font-size: 10pt">balance which is fully insured by the FDIC. The funds can be tracked by its primary financial
institution. New funds can be deposited and withdrawn from that single relationship. The Company believes the financial institutions
it uses are credit worthy and stable. The Company does not believe that it is exposed to any significant credit risk in cash and cash
equivalents or restricted cash.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="margin: 0; text-align: justify">The Company&#8217;s accounts receivable and revenue are significantly concentrated with governmental agencies, primarily
Medicare and Medicaid. As of December 31, 2024, approximately <span id="xdx_906_eus-gaap--ConcentrationRiskPercentage1_dp_uPure_c20240101__20241231__us-gaap--ConcentrationRiskByBenchmarkAxis__us-gaap--SalesRevenueNetMember__us-gaap--ConcentrationRiskByTypeAxis__us-gaap--ProductConcentrationRiskMember__srt--MajorCustomersAxis__custom--GovernmentalAgenciesPrimarilymedicareMedicaidMember_zE1smpCZeFOi" title="Concentration of credit risk percentage"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ConcentrationRiskPercentage1" contextRef="From2024-01-012024-12-31_us-gaap_SalesRevenueNetMember_us-gaap_ProductConcentrationRiskMember_custom_GovernmentalAgenciesPrimarilymedicareMedicaidMember" id="Fact000549" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" scale="-2" unitRef="Pure">77</ix:nonFraction></span>% of the Company&#8217;s accounts receivable and <span id="xdx_90B_eus-gaap--ConcentrationRiskPercentage1_dp_uPure_c20240101__20241231__us-gaap--ConcentrationRiskByBenchmarkAxis__us-gaap--SalesRevenueNetMember__us-gaap--ConcentrationRiskByTypeAxis__us-gaap--RevenueFromRightsConcentrationRiskMember__srt--MajorCustomersAxis__custom--GovernmentalAgenciesPrimarilymedicareMedicaidMember_zE00JZKB6Bk5" title="Concentration of credit risk percentage"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ConcentrationRiskPercentage1" contextRef="From2024-01-012024-12-31_us-gaap_SalesRevenueNetMember_us-gaap_RevenueFromRightsConcentrationRiskMember_custom_GovernmentalAgenciesPrimarilymedicareMedicaidMember" id="Fact000551" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" scale="-2" unitRef="Pure">82</ix:nonFraction></span>% of its revenue were
derived from these programs.</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0">&#160;</p>

</ix:nonNumeric><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" escape="true" id="Fact000553" name="us-gaap:PropertyPlantAndEquipmentPolicyTextBlock"><p id="xdx_843_eus-gaap--PropertyPlantAndEquipmentPolicyTextBlock_ztHvpoj16srk" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><b><span id="xdx_869_zYpjCe4uxm2f">Property and Equipment</span></b></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">In accordance
with purchase accounting guidance established for entities under common control, the property and equipment acquired from entities under
common control are stated at their carrying value on the date of acquisition. Property and equipment acquired from entities that are not under
common control is recorded at its estimated fair value. Estimated fair value is determined with the assistance from independent valuation
specialists using recent sales of similar assets, market conditions or projected cash flows of properties using standard industry valuation
techniques.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">Upon acquisition
of real estate properties determined to be asset acquisitions, the Company determines the total purchase price of each property and allocates
the purchase price of acquired properties to net tangible and identified intangible assets based on relative fair values. Fair value
estimates are based on information obtained from independent appraisals, other market data, and information obtained during due diligence
period. Acquisition-related costs such as due diligence, legal and accounting fees are included in the purchase price. Initial valuations
are subject to change during the measurement period, but the period ends as soon as the information is available. The measurement period
shall not exceed one year from the date of acquisition.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>


<ix:exclude><!-- Field: Page; Sequence: 54; Value: 1 -->
    <div style="border-bottom: Black 1pt solid; margin-bottom: 6pt"><table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; font-size: 10pt"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: left"><td style="text-align: center; width: 100%">F-<!-- Field: Sequence; Type: Arabic; Name: PageNo -->8<!-- Field: /Sequence --></td></tr></table></div>
    <div style="break-before: page; margin-top: 6pt"><table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; font-size: 10pt"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: left"><td style="text-align: center; width: 100%">&#160;</td></tr></table></div>
    <!-- Field: /Page --></ix:exclude>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">Upon acquisition
of business entities and real estate determined to be a business combination, the Company identifies and recognizes the net tangible
and identified intangible assets based on fair values, and net assets as goodwill or gain on bargain purchase. Fair value estimates are
based on information obtained from independent appraisals, other market data, information obtained during due diligence and information
related to the marketing, leasing, and or operating at the specific property. Acquisition-related costs such as due diligence, legal
and accounting fees are expensed as incurred. Initial valuations are subject to change during the measurement period, but the period
ends as soon as the information is available. The measurement period shall not exceed one year from the date of acquisition.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">Any subsequent
betterments and improvements are stated at historical cost. Depreciation is provided using the straight-line method over the estimated
useful lives of the assets, and tenant improvements are amortized over the remaining term of the lease. Useful lives of the assets
are summarized as follows:</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

</ix:nonNumeric><p id="xdx_8A2_zAr4gCIeDXl1" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

</ix:nonNumeric><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" escape="true" id="Fact000564" name="us-gaap:ImpairmentOrDisposalOfLongLivedAssetsPolicyTextBlock"><p id="xdx_84E_eus-gaap--ImpairmentOrDisposalOfLongLivedAssetsPolicyTextBlock_zN4jn5PK0Jtl" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><b><span id="xdx_860_zJX9ZNNa56q7">Impairment of Long-Lived
Assets</span></b></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">When circumstances
indicate the carrying value of property and equipment may not be recoverable, the Company reviews the property for impairment. This review
is based on an estimate of the future undiscounted cash flows, excluding interest charges, expected to result from the property&#8217;s
use and eventual disposition. This estimate considers factors such as expected future operating income, market and other applicable trends
and residual value, as well as the effects of leasing demand, competition, and other factors. If impairment exists, due to the inability
to recover the carrying amount of the property, an impairment loss is recorded to the extent that the carrying value exceeds the estimated
fair value of the property and equipment. Estimated fair value is determined with the assistance from independent valuation specialists
using recent sales of similar assets, market conditions or projected cash flows of the property using standard industry valuation techniques.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

</ix:nonNumeric><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" escape="true" id="Fact000566" name="us-gaap:DeferredChargesPolicyTextBlock"><p id="xdx_84A_eus-gaap--DeferredChargesPolicyTextBlock_zNbaKsWsE9sb" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><b><span id="xdx_866_zVWXFtu8FRBe">Debt
Issuance Costs</span></b></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">Debt issuance costs are amortized over the life of the related loan using the straight-line method, which approximates the effective interest
method. Amortization expense for the years ended December 31, 2024 and 2023 totaled $<span id="xdx_90C_eus-gaap--AmortizationOfIntangibleAssets_c20240101__20241231_zRQdW3RZOlD7" title="Amortization expenses"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:AmortizationOfIntangibleAssets" contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" id="Fact000568" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">103,432</ix:nonFraction></span> and $<span id="xdx_90C_eus-gaap--AmortizationOfIntangibleAssets_c20230101__20231231_zaaAYwZF3BZa" title="Amortization expenses"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:AmortizationOfIntangibleAssets" contextRef="From2023-01-012023-12-31" id="Fact000570" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">347,783</ix:nonFraction></span>, respectively. Deferred loan
cost amortization is included as a component of interest expense in the consolidated statements of operations.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

</ix:nonNumeric><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" escape="true" id="Fact000572" name="us-gaap:GoodwillAndIntangibleAssetsGoodwillPolicy"><p id="xdx_84D_eus-gaap--GoodwillAndIntangibleAssetsGoodwillPolicy_zreefNT9fwde" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><b><span id="xdx_86E_zFFwG9t5gO4i">Goodwill</span></b></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">Goodwill
represents the excess of the cost of an acquired business over the amounts assigned to its net assets. Goodwill is not amortized but
is tested for impairment at a reporting unit level on an annual basis or when an event occurs, or circumstances change that would more
likely than not reduce the fair value of a reporting unit below its carrying amount. Events or changes in circumstances that may trigger
interim impairment reviews include significant changes in business climate, operating results, planned investments in the reporting unit,
or an expectation that the carrying amount may not be recoverable, among other factors.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">The Company
may first assess qualitative factors to determine whether it is more likely than not that the fair value of a reporting unit is less
than its carrying amount. If, after assessing the totality of events and circumstances, the Company determines it is more likely than
not that the fair value of the reporting unit is greater than it&#8217;s carrying amount, an impairment test is unnecessary. If an impairment
test is necessary, the Company will estimate the fair value of its related reporting units. If the carrying value of a reporting unit
exceeds its fair value, the goodwill of that reporting unit is determined to be impaired, and the Company will proceed with recording
an impairment charge equal to the excess of the carrying value over the related fair value.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>


<ix:exclude><!-- Field: Page; Sequence: 55; Value: 1 -->
    <div style="border-bottom: Black 1pt solid; margin-bottom: 6pt"><table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; font-size: 10pt"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: left"><td style="text-align: center; width: 100%">F-<!-- Field: Sequence; Type: Arabic; Name: PageNo -->9<!-- Field: /Sequence --></td></tr></table></div>
    <div style="break-before: page; margin-top: 6pt"><table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; font-size: 10pt"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: left"><td style="text-align: center; width: 100%">&#160;</td></tr></table></div>
    <!-- Field: /Page --></ix:exclude>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">The Company
has recorded Goodwill in connection with business acquisitions during the year ended December 31, 2020. During the years
ended December 31, 2024 and 2023, the Company recorded <span id="xdx_903_eus-gaap--GoodwillImpairmentLoss_do_c20240101__20241231_z4evEEaFnd7e" title="Impairment on goodwill"><span id="xdx_907_eus-gaap--GoodwillImpairmentLoss_do_c20230101__20231231_zSt91qegXkve" title="Impairment on goodwill"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:GoodwillImpairmentLoss" contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" id="Fact000574" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" decimals="0" unitRef="USD"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:GoodwillImpairmentLoss" contextRef="From2023-01-012023-12-31" id="Fact000576" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">no</ix:nonFraction></ix:nonFraction></span></span> impairment of Goodwill.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

</ix:nonNumeric><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" escape="true" id="Fact000578" name="us-gaap:RevenueRecognitionPolicyTextBlock"><p id="xdx_840_eus-gaap--RevenueRecognitionPolicyTextBlock_zDSk7b0NtuX4" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><b><span id="xdx_863_zKcmL2ZlIGjf">Revenue Recognition</span></b></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">Rent receivables
are carried net of an allowance for uncollectible amounts. An allowance is maintained for estimated losses resulting from the inability
of certain tenants to meet the contractual obligations under their lease agreements.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">The
Company&#8217;s leases may be subject to annual escalations of the minimum monthly rent required under each lease. The accompanying
consolidated financial statements reflect rental income on a straight-line basis over the term of each lease. During the year ended
December 31, 2024 and 2023, the Company did not terminate leases that would result in annual escalations.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">When the
lessee is the owner of any improvements, any lessee improvement allowance that is funded by the Company is treated as a lease incentive
and amortized as a reduction of revenue over the lease term. As of December 31, 2024 and 2023, there were no deferred lease incentives
recorded.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">The
Company recognizes revenue in accordance with ASC 606, &#8220;<i>Revenue from Contracts with Customers (Topic 606),&#8221;</i>
including subsequently issued updates. Under the accounting guidance our revenues are presented net of estimated contractual
allowances, and we no longer present the contractual allowance as a separate line item on our balance sheet.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">The Company
reviews its calculations for the realizability of gross service revenues monthly to make certain that we are properly allowing for the
uncollectible portion of our gross billings and that our estimates remain sensitive to variances and changes within our payer groups.
The contractual allowance calculation is made based on historical allowance rates for the various specific payer groups monthly with
a greater emphasis given to current trends. This calculation is routinely analyzed by the Company based on actual allowances issued by
payers and the actual payments made to determine what adjustments, if any, are needed.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">Our revenues
generally relate to contracts with patients in which our performance obligations are to provide health care services to the patients.
Revenues are recorded during the period our obligations to provide health care services are satisfied. Our performance obligations for
inpatient services are generally satisfied over periods that average approximately five days, and revenues are recognized based on charges
incurred in relation to total expected charges. The contractual relationships with patients, in most cases, also involve a third-party
payer (Medicare and Medicaid) and the transaction prices for the services provided are dependent upon the terms provided by the third
party payer or payers. Medicare generally pays for inpatient and outpatient services at prospectively determined rates based on clinical,
diagnostic and other factors. Services provided to patients having Medicaid coverage are generally paid at prospectively determined rates
per discharge, per identified service or per covered member.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">Our revenues
are based upon the estimated amounts we expect to be entitled to receive from patients and third-party payers. Estimates of contractual
allowances under managed care are based upon the payment terms specified in the related contractual agreements.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>


<ix:exclude><!-- Field: Page; Sequence: 56; Value: 1 -->
    <div style="border-bottom: Black 1pt solid; margin-bottom: 6pt"><table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; font-size: 10pt"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: left"><td style="text-align: center; width: 100%">F-<!-- Field: Sequence; Type: Arabic; Name: PageNo -->10<!-- Field: /Sequence --></td></tr></table></div>
    <div style="break-before: page; margin-top: 6pt"><table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; font-size: 10pt"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: left"><td style="text-align: center; width: 100%">&#160;</td></tr></table></div>
    <!-- Field: /Page --></ix:exclude>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">Laws and
regulations governing the Medicare and Medicaid programs are complex and subject to interpretation. Estimated reimbursement amounts are
adjusted in subsequent periods as cost reports are prepared and filed and as final settlements are determined. In relation to certain
government programs, primarily Medicare, this is generally referred to as the &#8220;cost report&#8221; filing and settlement process.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

</ix:nonNumeric><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" escape="true" id="Fact000580" name="GBCS:ReceivablesTradeAndOtherAccountsReceivableAllowancesForDoubtfulAccountsPolicy"><p id="xdx_843_ecustom--ReceivablesTradeAndOtherAccountsReceivableAllowancesForDoubtfulAccountsPolicy_zrWQOgDBR7Ke" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><b><span id="xdx_86F_zH4xABFMBQhj">Allowance
for Credit Losses</span></b></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">In September
2016, the FASB issued ASU 2016-13, <i>Measurement of Credit Losses on Financial Instrument </i>(&#8220;ASU 2016-13&#8221;). ASU 2016-13
requires entities to use a forward-looking approach based on current expected credit losses (&#8220;CECL&#8221;) to estimate credit losses
on certain types of financial instruments, including trade receivables. This may result in the earlier recognition of allowances for
credit losses. The Company adopted ASU 2016-13 on January 1, 2023.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify">As
part of the analysis, the Company determined that all trade accounts receivable were of similar risk. Given the economy and our
services provided, we determined the trade accounts receivable would not be impacted. The Company recorded an allowance of $<span id="xdx_900_eus-gaap--AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivable_iI_pn5n6_c20241231_ziUfLLyBsMj6" title="Allowance for doubtful accounts receivable"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivable" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31" id="Fact000582" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5" scale="6" unitRef="USD">1,018,305</ix:nonFraction></span>
and $<span id="xdx_905_eus-gaap--AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivable_iI_pn5n6_c20231231_zFe8wsBq7Rj5" title="Allowance for doubtful accounts receivable"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivable" contextRef="AsOf2023-12-31" id="Fact000584" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5" scale="6" unitRef="USD">1,874,068</ix:nonFraction></span> as of December 31, 2024 and 2023, respectively. This included fully reserving for receivables greater than 90 days. For the year ended
December 31, 2024 and 2023, the Company recorded $<span id="xdx_905_eus-gaap--ProvisionForOtherCreditLosses_c20240101__20241231_zjH79n6sfEQ" title="Provision for credit loss"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ProvisionForOtherCreditLosses" contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" id="Fact000586" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">1,042,698</ix:nonFraction></span> and $<span id="xdx_90D_eus-gaap--ProvisionForOtherCreditLosses_c20230101__20231231_z0JVIx0Nn0Gi" title="Provision for credit loss"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ProvisionForOtherCreditLosses" contextRef="From2023-01-012023-12-31" id="Fact000588" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">2,733,157</ix:nonFraction></span>, respectively, on the statement of operations as a provision for credit losses
expense.</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">The
CARES Act provides an employee retention credit (&#8220;CARES Employee Retention Credit&#8221;), which is a refundable tax credit
against certain employment taxes of up to $<span id="xdx_90B_ecustom--EmploymentTax_c20240101__20241231__srt--RangeAxis__srt--MaximumMember__us-gaap--PlanNameAxis__custom--CARESActMember_z7GP2kmbpUj8" title="Employment taxes"><ix:nonFraction name="GBCS:EmploymentTax" contextRef="From2024-01-012024-12-31_srt_MaximumMember_custom_CARESActMember" id="Fact000590" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">5,000</ix:nonFraction></span>
per employee for eligible employers. The tax credit is equal to <span id="xdx_905_ecustom--PercentageOfEmployeeTaxCredit_pid_dp_uPure_c20200101__20201231__us-gaap--PlanNameAxis__custom--CARESActMember_zymJSkDxcZ94" title="Tax credit"><ix:nonFraction name="GBCS:PercentageOfEmployeeTaxCredit" contextRef="From2020-01-012020-12-31_custom_CARESActMember" id="Fact000592" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" scale="-2" unitRef="Pure">50</ix:nonFraction></span>%
of qualified wages paid to employees during a quarter, capped at $<span id="xdx_901_eus-gaap--SalariesAndWages_c20200101__20201231__us-gaap--PlanNameAxis__custom--CARESActMember_zq4ydsAtgF3j" title="Salaries and wages"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:SalariesAndWages" contextRef="From2020-01-012020-12-31_custom_CARESActMember" id="Fact000594" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">10,000</ix:nonFraction></span>
of qualified wages per employee through December 31, 2020. Additional relief provisions were passed by the United States government,
which extend and slightly expand the qualified wage caps on these credits through December 31, 2021. Based on these additional
provisions, the tax credit is now equal to <span id="xdx_902_ecustom--PercentageOfEmployeeTaxCredit_pid_dp_uPure_c20240101__20241231__us-gaap--PlanNameAxis__custom--CARESActMember_zMkiEquyEEi7" title="Percentage of employee tax credit"><ix:nonFraction name="GBCS:PercentageOfEmployeeTaxCredit" contextRef="From2024-01-012024-12-31_custom_CARESActMember" id="Fact000596" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" scale="-2" unitRef="Pure">70</ix:nonFraction></span>%
of qualified wages paid to employees during a quarter, and the limit on qualified wages per employee has been increased to $<span id="xdx_905_eus-gaap--SalariesAndWages_c20240101__20241231__us-gaap--PlanNameAxis__custom--CARESActMember_zfhOfnotlfYj" title="Salaries and wages"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:SalariesAndWages" contextRef="From2024-01-012024-12-31_custom_CARESActMember" id="Fact000598" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">10,000</ix:nonFraction></span>
of qualified wages per quarter. The Company qualified for the tax credit under the CARES Act for qualified wages for the years ended
December 31, 2020 and 2021. In February 2023, the Company submitted filings for CARES Employee Retention Credits totaling $<span id="xdx_900_ecustom--EmployeeRetentionCreditsReceivable_iI_pn5n6_c20230228_z0Ny3alCVjji" title="Employees retention credits"><ix:nonFraction name="GBCS:EmployeeRetentionCreditsReceivable" contextRef="AsOf2023-02-28" id="Fact000600" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5" scale="6" unitRef="USD">6.9</ix:nonFraction></span>
million. As of December 31, 2023, the Company recorded an employee retention credits receivable of approximately $<span id="xdx_906_ecustom--EmployeeRetentionCreditsReceivable_iI_pn5n6_c20241231__us-gaap--PlanNameAxis__custom--CARESActMember_z2IQUwReCsM1" title="Employee retention credits receivable"><span id="xdx_900_ecustom--EmployeeRetentionCreditsReceivable_iI_pn5n6_c20231231__us-gaap--PlanNameAxis__custom--CARESActMember_zIiBcqBhlgD1" title="Employee retention credits receivable"><ix:nonFraction name="GBCS:EmployeeRetentionCreditsReceivable" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_custom_CARESActMember" id="Fact000602" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5" scale="6" unitRef="USD"><ix:nonFraction name="GBCS:EmployeeRetentionCreditsReceivable" contextRef="AsOf2023-12-31_custom_CARESActMember" id="Fact000604" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5" scale="6" unitRef="USD">1.3</ix:nonFraction></ix:nonFraction></span></span>
million. Upon evaluation of the collectability of this receivable, the Company recorded a full allowance against this
receivable and recorded an expense to provision for credit losses on the statement of operations of $<span id="xdx_908_eus-gaap--PremiumsReceivableAllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsWriteOffsAgainstAllowance_c20230101__20231231_zLV1qoRPauU4" title="Expense and provision for credit loss"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:PremiumsReceivableAllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsWriteOffsAgainstAllowance" contextRef="From2023-01-012023-12-31" id="Fact000606" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">1,257,952</ix:nonFraction></span> for the year ended December 31, 2023. The Company has made no changes to these amounts as of and for
the year ended December 31, 2024.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt"></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

</ix:nonNumeric><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" escape="true" id="Fact000608" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationOptionAndIncentivePlansPolicy"><p id="xdx_84C_eus-gaap--ShareBasedCompensationOptionAndIncentivePlansPolicy_zLdtrettrgN2" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0"><b><span id="xdx_867_z9rf7dTcVYEd">Stock-Based Compensation</span></b></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0; text-align: justify">The Company accounts for stock-based compensation
in accordance with Accounting Standards Codification (&#8220;ASC&#8221;) ASC 718, &#8220;Compensation-Stock Compensation&#8221;. ASC 718
requires companies to measure the cost of employee services received in exchange for an award of equity instruments, including stock options,
based on the grant-date fair value of the award and to recognize it as compensation expense over the period the employee is required to
provide service in exchange for the award, usually the vesting period. During the years ended December 31, 2024 and 2023, the Company
recorded <span id="xdx_904_eus-gaap--EmployeeBenefitsAndShareBasedCompensation_dn_c20240101__20241231_zunZrS4tD4o7" title="Stock based compensation"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:EmployeeBenefitsAndShareBasedCompensation" contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" id="Fact000610" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">none</ix:nonFraction></span>&#160;and $<span id="xdx_901_eus-gaap--EmployeeBenefitsAndShareBasedCompensation_c20230101__20231231_zg9MeuOLmned" title="Stock based compensation"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:EmployeeBenefitsAndShareBasedCompensation" contextRef="From2023-01-012023-12-31" id="Fact000612" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">625</ix:nonFraction></span>, respectively, of stock-based compensation on the statement of operations.</p>
<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt"></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

</ix:nonNumeric><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" escape="true" id="Fact000614" name="us-gaap:FairValueMeasurementPolicyPolicyTextBlock"><p id="xdx_846_eus-gaap--FairValueMeasurementPolicyPolicyTextBlock_zvAa1n72AIt7" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><b><span id="xdx_86A_zqwkWCMPFAna">Fair
Value Measurements</span></b></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">The
Company utilizes the methods of fair value measurement as described in ASC 820 &#8220;Fair Value Measurement&#8221; (ASC 820) to
value its financial assets and liabilities. As defined in ASC 820, fair value is based on the price that would be received to sell
an asset or paid to transfer a liability in an orderly transaction between market participants at the measurement date. In order to
increase consistency and comparability in fair value measurements, ASC 820 establishes a fair value hierarchy that prioritizes
observable and unobservable inputs used to measure fair value into three broad levels, which are described below:</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">Level 1
&#8211; Quoted market prices in active markets for identical assets or liabilities at the measurement date.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>


<ix:exclude><!-- Field: Page; Sequence: 57; Value: 1 -->
    <div style="border-bottom: Black 1pt solid; margin-bottom: 6pt"><table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; font-size: 10pt"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: left"><td style="text-align: center; width: 100%">F-<!-- Field: Sequence; Type: Arabic; Name: PageNo -->11<!-- Field: /Sequence --></td></tr></table></div>
    <div style="break-before: page; margin-top: 6pt"><table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; font-size: 10pt"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: left"><td style="text-align: center; width: 100%">&#160;</td></tr></table></div>
    <!-- Field: /Page --></ix:exclude>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">Level 2
&#8211; Quoted prices for similar assets or liabilities in active markets; quoted prices for identical or similar assets and liabilities
in markets that are not active; or other inputs that are observable and can be corroborated by observable market data.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">Level 3
&#8211; Inputs reflecting management&#8217;s best estimates and assumptions of what market participants would use in pricing assets or
liabilities at the measurement date. The inputs are unobservable in the market and significant to the valuation of the instruments.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">A financial
instrument&#8217;s categorization within the valuation hierarchy is based upon the lowest level of input that is significant to the fair
value measurement.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">The Company
has no financial assets or financial liabilities that are required to be measured at fair value on a recurring basis as of December 31,
2024, and 2023.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">The carrying
values of cash and cash equivalents, accounts payable, accrued liabilities and other short-term debt, approximate their fair value because
of the short-term nature of these financial instruments. The carrying value of long-term debt approximates fair value since the related
rates of interest approximate current market rates.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">Upon acquisition
of real estate properties, the Company determines the total purchase price of each property and allocates this price based on the fair
value of the tangible assets and intangible assets, if any, acquired and any liabilities assumed based on Level 3 inputs. These Level
3 inputs can include comparable sales values, discount rates, and capitalization rates from a third-party appraisal or other market sources.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

</ix:nonNumeric><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" escape="true" id="Fact000616" name="us-gaap:IncomeTaxPolicyTextBlock"><p id="xdx_845_eus-gaap--IncomeTaxPolicyTextBlock_zhNMRBBBkDWj" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><b><span id="xdx_864_zbGHy1XaT7te">Income
Taxes</span></b></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">As previously
disclosed in the &#8220;Organization and Description of the Business&#8221; section of this Note, the Company&#8217;s focus has partially
shifted from leasing nursing home assets to independent operators toward owning and operating its real estate assets itself.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">The
Company uses the asset and liability method of accounting for income taxes in accordance with ASC 740 &#8220;Income Taxes&#8221;. Accordingly, deferred tax assets and liabilities are
recognized for the future tax consequences attributable to differences between the financial statement carrying amounts of assets
and liabilities and their respective tax bases. Deferred tax assets and liabilities are measured using enacted tax rates expected to
apply to taxable income in the years in which those temporary differences are expected to be recovered or settled. The effect on
deferred tax assets and liabilities of a change in tax rates resulting from new legislation is recognized in income in the period of
enactment. A valuation allowance is established against deferred tax assets when management concludes that the &#8220;more likely
than not&#8221; realization criteria has not been met. The Company recognizes the effect of income tax positions only if those
positions are more likely than not of being sustained.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

</ix:nonNumeric><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" escape="true" id="Fact000618" name="us-gaap:EarningsPerSharePolicyTextBlock"><p id="xdx_844_eus-gaap--EarningsPerSharePolicyTextBlock_zV0kAoAwpkj3" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><b><span id="xdx_86D_zQRKlLzbYfde">Loss Per Common Share</span></b></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">Basic earnings
per share are based on the weighted-average number of shares of common stock outstanding. FASB ASC Topic 260, &#8220;Earnings per Share&#8221;,
requires the Company to include additional shares in the computation of earnings per share, assuming dilution.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">Diluted
earnings per share are based on the assumption that all dilutive options and warrants were converted or exercised by applying the treasury
stock method and that all convertible preferred stock were converted into common shares by applying the if-converted method. Under the
treasury stock method, options and warrants are assumed to be exercised at the beginning of the period or at the time of issuance, if
later, and as if funds obtained thereby were used to purchase common stock at the average market price during the period. Under the if-converted
method, the preferred dividends applicable to convertible preferred stock are added back to the numerator. The convertible preferred
stock is assumed to have been converted at the beginning of the period or at time of issuance, if later, and the resulting common shares
are included in the denominator.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">We calculate
basic earnings per share by dividing net loss attributable to common stockholders (the &#8220;numerator&#8221;) by the weighted average
number of common shares outstanding (the &#8220;denominator&#8221;) during the reporting period. Diluted earnings per share is calculated
similarly but reflects the potential impact of outstanding options, warrants and other commitments to issue common stock, including shares
issuable upon the conversion of convertible preferred stock outstanding, except where the impact would be anti-dilutive.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>


<ix:exclude><!-- Field: Page; Sequence: 58; Value: 1 -->
    <div style="border-bottom: Black 1pt solid; margin-bottom: 6pt"><table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; font-size: 10pt"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: left"><td style="text-align: center; width: 100%">F-<!-- Field: Sequence; Type: Arabic; Name: PageNo -->12<!-- Field: /Sequence --></td></tr></table></div>
    <div style="break-before: page; margin-top: 6pt"><table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; font-size: 10pt"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: left"><td style="text-align: center; width: 100%">&#160;</td></tr></table></div>
    <!-- Field: /Page --></ix:exclude>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

</ix:nonNumeric><p id="xdx_8AD_zNXUsI6xMWij" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">Options
to purchase <span id="xdx_90A_eus-gaap--ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionsOutstandingNumber_iI_c20241231__us-gaap--StatementClassOfStockAxis__us-gaap--CommonStockMember_z3Sh7GA7kOub" title="Option to purchase shares"><span id="xdx_904_eus-gaap--ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionsOutstandingNumber_iI_c20231231__us-gaap--StatementClassOfStockAxis__us-gaap--CommonStockMember_zJWbqPcIh4Vd" title="Option to purchase shares"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionsOutstandingNumber" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_us-gaap_CommonStockMember15632000" id="Fact000654" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" unitRef="Shares"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionsOutstandingNumber" contextRef="AsOf2023-12-31_us-gaap_CommonStockMember15632000" id="Fact000656" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" unitRef="Shares">30,000</ix:nonFraction></ix:nonFraction></span></span> shares of common stock were outstanding during the years ended December 31, 2024 and 2023 but were not included in
the computation of diluted earnings per share because they are anti-dilutive due to the options&#8217; exercise price being greater than
the average market price of the common shares.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

</ix:nonNumeric><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" escape="true" id="Fact000658" name="us-gaap:SegmentReportingPolicyPolicyTextBlock"><p id="xdx_841_eus-gaap--SegmentReportingPolicyPolicyTextBlock_zn9tVnqgThQi" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><b><span id="xdx_869_zLLaCO7dUIQ6">Segment Reporting</span></b></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt"><span style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif; font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify"><span style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif; font-size: 10pt">The
Company operates through a single operating and reportable segment focused on the business of operating assisted living facilities, independent
living facilities, and skilled nursing facilities across the South and Southeastern portions of the US. The Company manages all business
activities on a consolidated basis. The Company&#8217;s chief operating decision maker (CODM) is the Chief Executive Officer.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify"><span style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif; font-size: 10pt">The
CODM evaluates the performance of the operating segment and allocates resources based on net loss that also is reported on the consolidated
statements of operations and comprehensive loss as net loss. The measure of the operating segment assets is reported on the consolidated
balance sheet as total assets.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify"><span style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif; font-size: 10pt">The
CODM uses net loss to monitor budget versus actual results and to analyze cash flows in assessing performance of the segment and allocating
resources. The significant expense categories regularly provided to the CODM include property taxes, insurance and other operating expenses
and general and administrative expenses. These expense categories are reported as separate line items in our consolidated statements
of operations and comprehensive loss. All our revenue is attributable to the United States and to our single operating segment.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt">&#160;</p>

</ix:nonNumeric><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" escape="true" id="Fact000660" name="us-gaap:NewAccountingPronouncementsPolicyPolicyTextBlock"><p id="xdx_84C_eus-gaap--NewAccountingPronouncementsPolicyPolicyTextBlock_zVQLbB5m7IM2" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><b><span id="xdx_866_zOHwhNI8eDj1">Recently
Adopted Accounting Pronouncements</span></b></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif; font-size: 10pt">In
October 2021, the FASB issued ASU 2021-08, Business Combinations (Topic 805): Accounting for Contract Assets and Contract Liabilities
from Contracts with Customers, which requires an acquirer to recognize and measure contract assets and liabilities acquired in a business
combination in accordance with Revenue from Contracts with Customers (Topic 606) rather than adjust them to fair value at the acquisition
date. This update is effective beginning with the Company&#8217;s 2024 fiscal year annual reporting period. The Company adopted ASU 2021-08
on January 1, 2024 on a prospective basis. The adoption of this standard did not have a material impact on the Company&#8217;s consolidated
financial statements.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">In November
2023, the FASB issued ASU 2023-07, Segment Reporting (Topic 280): Improvements to Reportable Segment Disclosures. This ASU requires public
entities to disclose information about their reportable segments&#8217; significant expenses and other segment items on an interim and
annual basis. Public entities with a single reportable segment are required to apply the disclosure requirements in ASU 2023-07, as well
as all existing segment disclosures and reconciliation requirements in ASC 280 on an interim and annual basis. ASU 2023-07 is effective
for fiscal years beginning after December 15, 2023, and for interim periods within fiscal years beginning after December 15, 2024, with
early adoption permitted. We adopted ASU 2023-07 for the fiscal year 2024. The impact of ASU 2023-07 resulted in additional disclosures
in our notes to the consolidated financial statements.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

</ix:nonNumeric><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" escape="true" id="Fact000662" name="GBCS:RecentlyIssuedAccountingPronouncementsNotYetAdoptedPolicyTextBlock"><p id="xdx_844_ecustom--RecentlyIssuedAccountingPronouncementsNotYetAdoptedPolicyTextBlock_zI4gmZjW808h" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><b><span id="xdx_869_z4li3YXIHkpl">Recently
issued Accounting Pronouncements Not Yet Adopted</span></b></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">In December
2023, the FASB issued ASU 2023-09, Income Taxes (Topic 740): Improvement to Income Tax Disclosures, amending income tax disclosure requirements
for the effective tax rate reconciliation and income taxes paid. The amendments in ASU 2023-09 are effective for fiscal years beginning
after December 15, 2024 and are applied prospectively. Early adoption and retrospective application of the amendments are permitted.
We are currently evaluating the impact of this update on our consolidated financial statements and disclosures.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">In November
2024, the FASB issued ASU No. 2024-03, Income Statement - Reporting Comprehensive Income - Expense Disaggregation (Subtopic 220-40):
Disaggregation of Income Statement Expenses. The amendments in ASU 2024-03 require a public business entity to disclose specific information
about certain costs and expenses in the notes to its financial statements for interim and annual reporting periods. The objective of
the disclosure requirements is to provide disaggregated information about a public business entity&#8217;s expenses to help investors
(i) better understand the entity&#8217;s performance, (ii) better assess the entity&#8217;s prospects for future cash flows, and (iii)
compare an entity&#8217;s performance over time and with that of other entities. ASU 2024-03 is effective for fiscal years beginning
after December 15, 2026, and for interim periods within fiscal years beginning after December 15, 2027, with early adoption permitted.
We are currently evaluating the impact of the adoption of ASU 2024-03.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">The FASB
and other entities issued new or modifications to, or interpretations of, existing accounting guidance during 2024. Management has carefully
considered the new pronouncements that altered generally accepted accounting principles and does not believe that any other new or modified
principles will have a material impact on the Company&#8217;s reported financial position or operations in the near term.</span></p>

</ix:nonNumeric><p id="xdx_856_zEF8vgYLiCt4" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

is a summary of the Company&#8217;s debt and debt &#8211; related parties outstanding as of December 31, 2024 and 2023:</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><span id="xdx_8B5_z1BjGpwyBryf" style="display: none">SCHEDULE OF DEBT OUTSTANDING</span></span></p>

</ix:nonNumeric><p id="xdx_8A9_zE08yZrAyBeg" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: center"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">The </span>weighted
average interest rate and term of our fixed rate debt are <span id="xdx_905_eus-gaap--DebtWeightedAverageInterestRate_iI_pid_dp_uPure_c20241231__us-gaap--VariableRateAxis__custom--FixedRateMember_zhlMKjsM1kbb" title="Debt weighted average interest rate"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DebtWeightedAverageInterestRate" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_custom_FixedRateMember" id="Fact000744" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" scale="-2" unitRef="Pure">4.68</ix:nonFraction></span>% and <span id="xdx_90F_eus-gaap--DebtInstrumentTerm_dtY_c20240101__20241231__us-gaap--VariableRateAxis__custom--FixedRateMember_zGXpEoHtRpP3" title="Debt instrument term"><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-012024-12-31_custom_FixedRateMember" format="ixt-sec:duryear" id="Fact000746" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentTerm">14.04</ix:nonNumeric></span> years, respectively, as of December 31, 2024. average interest rate and term of our variable rate debt are <span id="xdx_90B_eus-gaap--DebtWeightedAverageInterestRate_iI_pid_dp_uPure_c20241231__us-gaap--VariableRateAxis__custom--VariableRateMember_zsHQUUzRDWZk" title="Debt weighted average interest rate"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DebtWeightedAverageInterestRate" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_custom_VariableRateMember" id="Fact000748" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" scale="-2" unitRef="Pure">9.10</ix:nonFraction></span>% and <span id="xdx_904_eus-gaap--DebtInstrumentTerm_dtY_c20240101__20241231__us-gaap--VariableRateAxis__custom--VariableRateMember_zcqHeR7nLt0e" title="Debt instrument term"><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-012024-12-31_custom_VariableRateMember" format="ixt-sec:duryear" id="Fact000750" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentTerm">13.11</ix:nonNumeric></span> years, respectively, as of December 31, 2024.</p>

variable rate debt are <span id="xdx_905_eus-gaap--DebtWeightedAverageInterestRate_iI_pid_dp_uPure_c20231231__us-gaap--VariableRateAxis__custom--VariableRateMember_zLSAeyc3uhu2" title="Debt weighted average interest rate"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DebtWeightedAverageInterestRate" contextRef="AsOf2023-12-31_custom_VariableRateMember" id="Fact000756" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" scale="-2" unitRef="Pure">5.75</ix:nonFraction></span>% and <span id="xdx_908_eus-gaap--DebtInstrumentTerm_dtY_c20230101__20231231__us-gaap--VariableRateAxis__custom--VariableRateMember_zrOU9wHD2cQ" title="Debt instrument term"><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2023-01-012023-12-31_custom_VariableRateMember" format="ixt-sec:duryear" id="Fact000758" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentTerm">14.11</ix:nonNumeric></span> years, respectively, as of December 31, 2023.</p>

Senior and Senior Secured Promissory Notes</b></span></p>

Secured Notes</i></span></p>

31, 2024 and 2023, the senior secured notes are subject to annual interest of <span id="xdx_902_eus-gaap--DebtInstrumentInterestRateStatedPercentage_iI_pid_dp_uPure_c20231231__srt--RangeAxis__srt--MaximumMember__us-gaap--LongtermDebtTypeAxis__custom--SeniorSecuredNotesMember_zlcqI9Bw32Mj"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentInterestRateStatedPercentage" contextRef="AsOf2023-12-31_srt_MaximumMember_custom_SeniorSecuredNotesMember" id="Fact000759" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" scale="-2" unitRef="Pure">11</ix:nonFraction></span></span><span style="font-size: 10pt">%
with an original maturity date of <span id="xdx_904_eus-gaap--DebtInstrumentMaturityDate_c20230101__20231231__us-gaap--LongtermDebtTypeAxis__custom--SeniorSecuredNotesMember_zcCu6Uz8fz88"><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2023-01-012023-12-31_custom_SeniorSecuredNotesMember" format="ixt:datemonthdayyearen" id="Fact000760" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentMaturityDate">October
31, 2021</ix:nonNumeric></span></span><span style="font-size: 10pt">.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">In October
2018, the Company, through a registered broker-dealer acting as Placement Agent, undertook a private offering to accredited investors
of Units, each Unit consisting of an <span id="xdx_903_eus-gaap--DebtInstrumentInterestRateStatedPercentage_iI_pid_dp_c20181031__us-gaap--DebtInstrumentAxis__custom--ElevenPercentageSeniorSecuredNotesMember_zt9caidshb4j" title="Debt instrument, interest rate"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentInterestRateStatedPercentage" contextRef="AsOf2018-10-31_custom_ElevenPercentageSeniorSecuredNotesMember" id="Fact000836" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" scale="-2" unitRef="Pure">11</ix:nonFraction></span>% Senior Secured Note, due in <span id="xdx_903_eus-gaap--DebtInstrumentTerm_dc_c20181001__20181031__us-gaap--DebtInstrumentAxis__custom--ElevenPercentageSeniorSecuredNotesMember_zbGB8U6x0m8g" title="Debt term"><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2018-10-012018-10-31_custom_ElevenPercentageSeniorSecuredNotesMember" format="ixt-sec:durwordsen" id="Fact000838" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentTerm">three years</ix:nonNumeric></span>, (<span id="xdx_90F_eus-gaap--DebtInstrumentMaturityDate_dd_c20181001__20181031__us-gaap--DebtInstrumentAxis__custom--ElevenPercentageSeniorSecuredNotesMember_zOGzZeOuSrwg" title="Debt instrument maturity date"><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2018-10-012018-10-31_custom_ElevenPercentageSeniorSecuredNotesMember" format="ixt:datemonthdayyearen" id="Fact000840" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentMaturityDate">October 31, 2021</ix:nonNumeric></span>) and <span id="xdx_900_ecustom--ClassOfWarrantOrRightTitleOfSecurityWarrantOrRightsOutstanding_c20181001__20181031__us-gaap--DebtInstrumentAxis__custom--ElevenPercentageSeniorSecuredNotesMember_zhNz5uSEeJe" title="Warrants description"><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2018-10-012018-10-31_custom_ElevenPercentageSeniorSecuredNotesMember" id="Fact000842" name="GBCS:ClassOfWarrantOrRightTitleOfSecurityWarrantOrRightsOutstanding">one Warrant for each $10.00
in principal amount of Note exercisable for three years to purchase a share of Common Stock at an exercise price of $<span id="xdx_907_eus-gaap--ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1_iI_pid_c20181031__us-gaap--DebtInstrumentAxis__custom--ElevenPercentageSeniorSecuredNotesMember_zqztsHs09EC9" title="Warrants exercise price"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" contextRef="AsOf2018-10-31_custom_ElevenPercentageSeniorSecuredNotesMember" id="Fact000844" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" unitRef="USDPShares">5.00</ix:nonFraction></span> per share.</ix:nonNumeric></span>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-family: Times New Roman, Times, Serif; font-size: 10pt">These
notes were extended to <span id="xdx_902_ecustom--DebtInstrumentExtendedMaturityDate_c20220101__20221231__us-gaap--LongtermDebtTypeAxis__custom--SeniorSecuredNotesMember_ziGLLnZr09Ji" title="Extended maturity date"><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2022-01-012022-12-31_custom_SeniorSecuredNotesMember" format="ixt:datemonthdayyearen" id="Fact000895" name="GBCS:DebtInstrumentExtendedMaturityDate">June 30, 2023</ix:nonNumeric></span> and as consideration the Company modified the outstanding warrants to extend the life an additional
<span id="xdx_903_eus-gaap--WarrantsAndRightsOutstandingTerm_iI_dtY_c20221231__us-gaap--LongtermDebtTypeAxis__custom--SeniorSecuredNotesMember_zAzcfhJncwq6" title="Warrants additional life term"><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="AsOf2022-12-31_custom_SeniorSecuredNotesMember" format="ixt-sec:duryear" id="Fact000897" name="us-gaap:WarrantsAndRightsOutstandingTerm">1.67</ix:nonNumeric></span> years. As a result of the warrant modification, the Company recorded the incremental increase in fair value of $<span id="xdx_909_eus-gaap--AmortizationOfDebtDiscountPremium_c20220101__20221231__us-gaap--LongtermDebtTypeAxis__custom--SeniorSecuredNotesMember_z1C90BDroAQl" title="Amortization of debt discount"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:AmortizationOfDebtDiscountPremium" contextRef="From2022-01-012022-12-31_custom_SeniorSecuredNotesMember" id="Fact000899" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">844,425</ix:nonFraction></span> as a debt
discount which will be amortized over the new life of the notes.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">Effective
June 27, 2023, pursuant to an Allonge and Modification Agreement a Majority in Interest of the senior secured note holders agreed to
extend the maturity date of the notes to <span id="xdx_901_eus-gaap--DebtInstrumentMaturityDate_dd_c20230627__20230627__us-gaap--LongtermDebtTypeAxis__custom--SeniorSecuredNotesMember_zmDdtMMRs46i" title="Maturity date"><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2023-06-272023-06-27_custom_SeniorSecuredNotesMember" format="ixt:datemonthdayyearen" id="Fact000901" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentMaturityDate">December 31, 2024</ix:nonNumeric></span>, relying upon an Agreement Among Lenders to which all noteholders are a party.
As consideration effective July 1, 2023, the annual interest rate increased to <span id="xdx_901_eus-gaap--DebtInstrumentInterestRateEffectivePercentage_iI_pid_dp_uPure_c20230701__us-gaap--LongtermDebtTypeAxis__custom--SeniorSecuredNotesMember_zPfC7b8db3c2" title="Debt instrument, interest rate, effective percentage"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentInterestRateEffectivePercentage" contextRef="AsOf2023-07-01_custom_SeniorSecuredNotesMember" id="Fact000903" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" scale="-2" unitRef="Pure">11</ix:nonFraction></span>% and the Company issued a new warrant for every $<span id="xdx_90E_eus-gaap--WarrantsAndRightsOutstanding_iI_c20230701__us-gaap--LongtermDebtTypeAxis__custom--SeniorSecuredNotesMember_zTElwcvHhnDh" title="Warrant issued value"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:WarrantsAndRightsOutstanding" contextRef="AsOf2023-07-01_custom_SeniorSecuredNotesMember" id="Fact000905" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">10</ix:nonFraction></span>
in principal totaling <span id="xdx_90E_eus-gaap--ClassOfWarrantOrRightOutstanding_iI_pid_c20230701__us-gaap--LongtermDebtTypeAxis__custom--SeniorSecuredNotesMember_zuqGsMQZ1Sek" title="Number of new warrants"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightOutstanding" contextRef="AsOf2023-07-01_custom_SeniorSecuredNotesMember" id="Fact000907" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" unitRef="Shares">177,500</ix:nonFraction></span> of new warrants with an exercise price of $<span id="xdx_90F_eus-gaap--ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1_iI_pid_uUSDPShares_c20230701__us-gaap--LongtermDebtTypeAxis__custom--SeniorSecuredNotesMember_zTu3Y6GlLYP8" title="Warrants exercise price"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" contextRef="AsOf2023-07-01_custom_SeniorSecuredNotesMember" id="Fact000909" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" unitRef="USDPShares">5</ix:nonFraction></span> and an expiration date of <span id="xdx_907_eus-gaap--WarrantsAndRightsOutstandingMaturityDate_iI_dd_c20230701__us-gaap--LongtermDebtTypeAxis__custom--SeniorSecuredNotesMember_zhJLEo5YUzEh" title="Warrant maturity date"><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="AsOf2023-07-01_custom_SeniorSecuredNotesMember" format="ixt:datemonthdayyearen" id="Fact000911" name="us-gaap:WarrantsAndRightsOutstandingMaturityDate">December 31, 2024</ix:nonNumeric></span>. As a result of
the new warrants, the Company recorded the incremental increase in fair value of $<span id="xdx_90F_eus-gaap--AmortizationOfDebtDiscountPremium_c20230701__20230701__us-gaap--LongtermDebtTypeAxis__custom--SeniorSecuredNotesMember_zgbqgmCVAGq7" title="Amortization of debt discount"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:AmortizationOfDebtDiscountPremium" contextRef="From2023-07-012023-07-01_custom_SeniorSecuredNotesMember" id="Fact000913" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">84,352</ix:nonFraction></span> as a debt discount which is being amortized
over the life of the notes.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">Effective
December 31, 2024, pursuant to the Second Amended and Restated Allonge and Modification Agreement a Majority in Interest of the senior
secured note holders agreed to extend the maturity date of the notes to <span id="xdx_908_eus-gaap--DebtInstrumentMaturityDate_dd_c20240101__20241231_z0u3j0eJZr66" title="Maturity date"><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" format="ixt:datemonthdayyearen" id="Fact000915" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentMaturityDate">December 31, 2025</ix:nonNumeric></span>, relying upon an Agreement Among Lenders to
which all noteholders are a party. As consideration effective January 1, 2025, the annual interest rate increased to <span id="xdx_90B_eus-gaap--DebtInstrumentInterestRateEffectivePercentage_iI_pid_dp_uPure_c20241231__us-gaap--LongtermDebtTypeAxis__custom--SeniorSecuredNotesMember_z49Aq0qZ7Dd5" title="Debt instrument, interest rate, effective percentage"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentInterestRateEffectivePercentage" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_custom_SeniorSecuredNotesMember" id="Fact000917" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" scale="-2" unitRef="Pure">13</ix:nonFraction></span>% and the Company
extended the <span id="xdx_90D_eus-gaap--ClassOfWarrantOrRightOutstanding_iI_pid_c20241231__us-gaap--LongtermDebtTypeAxis__custom--SeniorSecuredNotesMember_zAcxUbLyapVd" title="Number of new warrants"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightOutstanding" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_custom_SeniorSecuredNotesMember" id="Fact000919" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" unitRef="Shares">177,500</ix:nonFraction></span> warrants previously issued with an original exercise price of $<span id="xdx_907_eus-gaap--ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1_iI_pid_uUSDPShares_c20241231__us-gaap--LongtermDebtTypeAxis__custom--SeniorSecuredNotesMember_zGtFR367dvrb" title="Warrants exercise price"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_custom_SeniorSecuredNotesMember" id="Fact000921" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" unitRef="USDPShares">5</ix:nonFraction></span> with a new expiration date of <span id="xdx_908_eus-gaap--WarrantsAndRightsOutstandingMaturityDate_iI_dd_c20241231__us-gaap--LongtermDebtTypeAxis__custom--SeniorSecuredNotesMember_zP9MQYHVAw89" title="Warrant maturity date"><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_custom_SeniorSecuredNotesMember" format="ixt:datemonthdayyearen" id="Fact000923" name="us-gaap:WarrantsAndRightsOutstandingMaturityDate">December 31, 2025</ix:nonNumeric></span>
and new exercise price of $<span id="xdx_90E_eus-gaap--ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1_iI_pid_c20241231__us-gaap--DebtInstrumentAxis__custom--SeniorSecuredNotesMember_zJYa4UXnc9nj" title="Warrants exercise price"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_custom_SeniorSecuredNotesMember15633828" id="Fact000925" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" unitRef="USDPShares">2.25</ix:nonFraction></span>.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">The Company
evaluated the Amendment Agreement and the amendment was not required to be accounted for as a Troubled Debt Restructuring under ASC 470-60
as no concession was granted to the Company. The Company then evaluated the Second Amended and Restated Allonge and Modification Agreement
was not required to be accounted for as an extinguishment under ASC 470-50, Debt &#8211; Modifications and Extinguishment. The Company
recorded the debt as a modification. As a result of the new warrants, the Company recorded the incremental increase in fair value as
a debt discount which is being amortized over the extended life of the notes of twelve months.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt"></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt"></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0; text-align: justify"></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0; text-align: justify"></p>

<p style="margin: 0; text-align: justify"></p>
<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt"></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: left"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><i>Promissory
Note</i></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">On March
29, 2023, the Company entered a short-term subordinated secured promissory note of $<span id="xdx_906_eus-gaap--SecuredDebtCurrent_iI_c20230329__us-gaap--LongtermDebtTypeAxis__custom--SecuredPromissoryNotesMember_zC5HxdHJIkt9" title="Secured promissory note"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:SecuredDebtCurrent" contextRef="AsOf2023-03-29_custom_SecuredPromissoryNotesMember" id="Fact000927" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">501,006</ix:nonFraction></span>. This note accrued interest at <span id="xdx_902_eus-gaap--DebtInstrumentInterestRateStatedPercentage_iI_dp_uPure_c20230329__us-gaap--LongtermDebtTypeAxis__custom--SecuredPromissoryNotesMember_zinrFChVM7cc" title="Interest percentage"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentInterestRateStatedPercentage" contextRef="AsOf2023-03-29_custom_SecuredPromissoryNotesMember" id="Fact000929" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" scale="-2" unitRef="Pure">6.75</ix:nonFraction></span>% and
originally matured on July 5, 2023. The Company extended this note to <span id="xdx_90D_ecustom--DebtInstrumentExtendedMaturityDate_dd_c20230905__20230905__us-gaap--LongtermDebtTypeAxis__custom--SecuredPromissoryNotesMember_zr3XsDeFc7G7" title="Extended maturity date"><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2023-09-052023-09-05_custom_SecuredPromissoryNotesMember" format="ixt:datemonthdayyearen" id="Fact000931" name="GBCS:DebtInstrumentExtendedMaturityDate">September 5, 2023</ix:nonNumeric></span>, accruing interest at <span id="xdx_90B_eus-gaap--DebtInstrumentInterestRateStatedPercentage_iI_dp_uPure_c20230905__us-gaap--LongtermDebtTypeAxis__custom--SecuredPromissoryNotesMember_zXcY9Ch2csol" title="Interest percentage"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentInterestRateStatedPercentage" contextRef="AsOf2023-09-05_custom_SecuredPromissoryNotesMember" id="Fact000933" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" scale="-2" unitRef="Pure">7.5</ix:nonFraction></span>%. This note and all
accrued interest was repaid on September 5, 2023.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>
