<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><b>Mortgage
Loans and Lines of Credit Secured by Real Estate</b></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">Mortgage
loans and other debts such as lines of credit are collateralized by all assets of each nursing home property and an assignment of its
rents. Collateral for certain mortgage loans includes the personal guarantee of a former but no longer related party, or corporate guarantees.
Mortgage loans for the periods presented consisted of the following:</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><b>Subordinated, Corporate,
and Other Debt</b></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" escape="true" id="Fact001018" name="GBCS:ScheduleOfOtherDebtTableTextBlock"><p id="xdx_899_ecustom--ScheduleOfOtherDebtTableTextBlock_zHyIZo1vsWXl" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">Other debt
due at December 31, 2024 and 2023 includes unsecured notes payable issued to entities controlled by the Company used to facilitate the
acquisition of the nursing home properties.</span></p>

</ix:nonNumeric><p id="xdx_8A1_zqusaVDpwfa8" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><b>Lines
of Credits</b></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">On April
12, 2024, the Company entered into a Commercial Line of Credit Agreement and Note with Southern Bank for a secured line of credit in
the principal amount limit of $<span id="xdx_90F_eus-gaap--DebtInstrumentFaceAmount_iI_c20240412__us-gaap--TypeOfArrangementAxis__custom--CommercialLineOfCreditAgreementMember_zaOILHy3NoB9" title="Debt instrument, face amount"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentFaceAmount" contextRef="AsOf2024-04-12_custom_CommercialLineOfCreditAgreementMember" id="Fact001089" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">750,000</ix:nonFraction></span> at a fixed interest rate of <span id="xdx_901_eus-gaap--DebtInstrumentInterestRateStatedPercentage_iI_pid_dp_uPure_c20240412__us-gaap--TypeOfArrangementAxis__custom--CommercialLineOfCreditAgreementMember_zoumIrfdqBh" title="Debt instrument, interest rate, stated percentage"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentInterestRateStatedPercentage" contextRef="AsOf2024-04-12_custom_CommercialLineOfCreditAgreementMember" id="Fact001091" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" scale="-2" unitRef="Pure">8.50</ix:nonFraction></span>% per annum with a Maturity Date of <span id="xdx_907_eus-gaap--DebtInstrumentMaturityDate_dd_c20240412__20240412__us-gaap--TypeOfArrangementAxis__custom--CommercialLineOfCreditAgreementMember_zGpbuNe5k6Af" title="Debt instrument, maturity date"><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-04-122024-04-12_custom_CommercialLineOfCreditAgreementMember" format="ixt:datemonthdayyearen" id="Fact001093" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentMaturityDate">April 12, 2025</ix:nonNumeric></span>. In October
2024, the Company entered into another Commercial Line of Credit Agreement and Note with Southern Bank for a secured line of credit in
the principal amount limit of $<span id="xdx_908_eus-gaap--DebtInstrumentFaceAmount_iI_c20241031__us-gaap--TypeOfArrangementAxis__custom--CommercialLineOfCreditAgreementMember_zJq7vB4y5ECb" title="Debt instrument, face amount"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentFaceAmount" contextRef="AsOf2024-10-31_custom_CommercialLineOfCreditAgreementMember" id="Fact001095" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">750,000</ix:nonFraction></span>&#160;at a fixed interest rate of&#160;<span id="xdx_906_eus-gaap--DebtInstrumentInterestRateStatedPercentage_iI_pid_dp_uPure_c20241031__us-gaap--TypeOfArrangementAxis__custom--CommercialLineOfCreditAgreementMember_zGxwkjYTqBw3" title="Debt instrument, interest rate, stated percentage"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentInterestRateStatedPercentage" contextRef="AsOf2024-10-31_custom_CommercialLineOfCreditAgreementMember" id="Fact001097" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" scale="-2" unitRef="Pure">8.50</ix:nonFraction></span>% per annum with a Maturity Date of&#160;<span id="xdx_903_eus-gaap--DebtInstrumentMaturityDate_dd_c20241001__20241031__us-gaap--TypeOfArrangementAxis__custom--CommercialLineOfCreditAgreementMember_z2wRMpgutObb" title="Debt instrument, maturity date"><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-10-012024-10-31_custom_CommercialLineOfCreditAgreementMember" format="ixt:datemonthdayyearen" id="Fact001099" name="us-gaap:DebtInstrumentMaturityDate">October 31,
2025</ix:nonNumeric></span>. As of December 31, 2024, the balance outstanding on the lines of credits are $<span id="xdx_906_eus-gaap--LineOfCredit_iI_c20241231__us-gaap--TypeOfArrangementAxis__custom--CommercialLineOfCreditAgreementMember_zzWRH9VjXCi9" title="Line of credit"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:LineOfCredit" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_custom_CommercialLineOfCreditAgreementMember" id="Fact001101" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">799,752</ix:nonFraction></span>&#160;and the amount available is approximately
$<span id="xdx_900_eus-gaap--LineOfCreditFacilityFairValueOfAmountOutstanding_iI_c20241231__us-gaap--TypeOfArrangementAxis__custom--CommercialLineOfCreditAgreementMember_zMXxiB0w1BZ" title="Line of credit amount available"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:LineOfCreditFacilityFairValueOfAmountOutstanding" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_custom_CommercialLineOfCreditAgreementMember" id="Fact001103" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">700,000</ix:nonFraction></span>.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

of Debt Discount</b></span></p>

expense for debt issuance costs and debt discounts totaled $<span id="xdx_90F_eus-gaap--AdjustmentForAmortization_pp0p0_c20240101__20241231_zjYsfiPD6e4d" title="Amortization expense"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:AdjustmentForAmortization" contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" id="Fact001105" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" scale="0" unitRef="USD">103,432</ix:nonFraction></span> and $<span id="xdx_903_eus-gaap--AdjustmentForAmortization_pp0p0_c20230101__20231231_zpAC1qNL6PF4" title="Amortization expense"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:AdjustmentForAmortization" contextRef="From2023-01-012023-12-31" id="Fact001107" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" scale="0" unitRef="USD">347,783</ix:nonFraction></span> for the years ended December 31, 2024 and 2023, respectively.</span></p>

</ix:nonNumeric><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" escape="true" id="Fact001123" name="us-gaap:StockholdersEquityNoteDisclosureTextBlock"><p id="xdx_802_eus-gaap--StockholdersEquityNoteDisclosureTextBlock_zdmfXk5uwVDh" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><b>4. <span id="xdx_82D_zXtQagRktot3">STOCKHOLDERS&#8217; EQUITY</span></b></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><b>Preferred Stock</b></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">The Company
has authorized <span id="xdx_90B_eus-gaap--PreferredStockSharesAuthorized_iI_c20241231_zgFJR9dJRfmj" title="Preferred Stock, Shares Authorized"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:PreferredStockSharesAuthorized" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31" id="Fact001125" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" unitRef="Shares">10,000,000</ix:nonFraction></span> shares of preferred stock. These shares may be issued in series with such rights and preferences as may be
determined by the board of directors.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><b>Series A Convertible Redeemable
Preferred Stock</b></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">The Company&#8217;s
Board of Directors has authorized <span id="xdx_902_eus-gaap--PreferredStockSharesAuthorized_iI_c20241231__us-gaap--StatementClassOfStockAxis__us-gaap--SeriesAPreferredStockMember_zMPwDhbn2J3j" title="Preferred stock, shares authorized"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:PreferredStockSharesAuthorized" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_us-gaap_SeriesAPreferredStockMember" id="Fact001127" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" unitRef="Shares">2,000,000</ix:nonFraction></span> shares of $<span id="xdx_901_eus-gaap--PreferredStockParOrStatedValuePerShare_iI_c20241231__us-gaap--StatementClassOfStockAxis__us-gaap--SeriesAPreferredStockMember_zCGLkS0axwX1" title="Preferred stock, par value"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:PreferredStockParOrStatedValuePerShare" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_us-gaap_SeriesAPreferredStockMember" id="Fact001129" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" unitRef="USDPShares">2.00</ix:nonFraction></span> stated value, Series A Preferred Stock. The preferred stock has a senior
liquidation preference value of $<span id="xdx_90E_eus-gaap--PreferredStockLiquidationPreference_iI_c20241231__us-gaap--StatementClassOfStockAxis__us-gaap--SeriesAPreferredStockMember_z51az0XWEF6c" title="Liquidation preference value"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:PreferredStockLiquidationPreference" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_us-gaap_SeriesAPreferredStockMember" id="Fact001131" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" unitRef="USDPShares">2.00</ix:nonFraction></span> per share and does not bear dividends.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">As of December
31, 2024 and 2023, the Company has <span id="xdx_90B_eus-gaap--PreferredStockSharesOutstanding_iI_c20241231__us-gaap--StatementClassOfStockAxis__us-gaap--SeriesAPreferredStockMember_zhtLuWiG4eEc" title="Preferred stock, shares outstanding"><span id="xdx_90D_eus-gaap--PreferredStockSharesOutstanding_iI_c20231231__us-gaap--StatementClassOfStockAxis__us-gaap--SeriesAPreferredStockMember_zFyJ9XNdO1t3" title="Preferred stock, shares outstanding"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:PreferredStockSharesOutstanding" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_us-gaap_SeriesAPreferredStockMember" id="Fact001133" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" unitRef="Shares"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:PreferredStockSharesOutstanding" contextRef="AsOf2023-12-31_us-gaap_SeriesAPreferredStockMember" id="Fact001135" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" unitRef="Shares">200,500</ix:nonFraction></ix:nonFraction></span></span> shares of Series A Preferred Stock outstanding.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><b>Series
D Convertible Preferred Stock</b></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">The Company
has established a class of preferred stock designated Series D Convertible Preferred Stock (&#8220;Series D preferred stock&#8221;) and
authorized an aggregate of <span id="xdx_90D_eus-gaap--PreferredStockSharesAuthorized_iI_c20241231__us-gaap--StatementClassOfStockAxis__us-gaap--SeriesDPreferredStockMember_zB3iDGVcEYTb" title="Preferred stock, shares authorized"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:PreferredStockSharesAuthorized" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_us-gaap_SeriesDPreferredStockMember" id="Fact001137" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" unitRef="Shares">1,000,000</ix:nonFraction></span> non-voting shares with a stated value of $<span id="xdx_90E_eus-gaap--PreferredStockParOrStatedValuePerShare_iI_c20241231__us-gaap--StatementClassOfStockAxis__us-gaap--SeriesDPreferredStockMember_zPGUJFVTienh" title="Preferred stock, par value"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:PreferredStockParOrStatedValuePerShare" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_us-gaap_SeriesDPreferredStockMember" id="Fact001139" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" unitRef="USDPShares">1.00</ix:nonFraction></span> per share. Holders of the Series D preferred stock
are entitled to receive dividends at the annual rate of <span id="xdx_906_eus-gaap--PreferredStockDividendRatePercentage_pid_dp_c20240101__20241231__us-gaap--StatementClassOfStockAxis__us-gaap--SeriesDPreferredStockMember_zJItjgMhZ9Ff" title="Preferred stock, dividend rate, percentage"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:PreferredStockDividendRatePercentage" contextRef="From2024-01-012024-12-31_us-gaap_SeriesDPreferredStockMember" id="Fact001141" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" scale="-2" unitRef="Pure">8</ix:nonFraction></span>% based on the stated value per share computed on the basis of a 360-day year
and twelve 30-day months. Dividends are cumulative, shall be declared quarterly, and are calculated from the date of issue and payable
on the 15th day of April, July, October, and January. The dividends may be paid, at the option of the holder either in cash or by the
issuance of shares of the Company&#8217;s common stock valued at the market price on the dividend record date. <span id="xdx_901_eus-gaap--PreferredStockCallOrExerciseFeatures_c20240101__20241231__us-gaap--StatementClassOfStockAxis__us-gaap--SeriesDPreferredStockMember_zWtm08hY3Ial" title="Preferred stock, call or exercise features"><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-012024-12-31_us-gaap_SeriesDPreferredStockMember" id="Fact001143" name="us-gaap:PreferredStockCallOrExerciseFeatures">Shares of the Series D
preferred stock are redeemable at the Company&#8217;s option. At the option of the holder, shares of the Series D preferred stock plus
any declared and unpaid dividends are convertible to shares of the Company&#8217;s common stock at a conversion rate of $<span id="xdx_901_eus-gaap--PreferredStockConvertibleConversionPrice_iI_c20241231__us-gaap--StatementClassOfStockAxis__us-gaap--SeriesDPreferredStockMember_zf5U2bIrhXEf" title="Conversion price per share"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:PreferredStockConvertibleConversionPrice" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_us-gaap_SeriesDPreferredStockMember" id="Fact001145" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" unitRef="USDPShares">1.00</ix:nonFraction></span> per share.</ix:nonNumeric></span></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">As of December
31, 2024, and 2023 the Company had <span id="xdx_906_eus-gaap--PreferredStockSharesOutstanding_iI_c20241231__us-gaap--StatementClassOfStockAxis__us-gaap--SeriesDPreferredStockMember_zkGrA1tT24mh" title="Preferred stock, shares outstanding"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:PreferredStockSharesOutstanding" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_us-gaap_SeriesDPreferredStockMember" id="Fact001147" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" unitRef="Shares">375,000</ix:nonFraction></span> shares of Series D Preferred Stock outstanding.</span></p>

years ended December 31, 2024 and 2023, the Company declared $<span id="xdx_90F_eus-gaap--DividendsPreferredStock_c20240101__20241231__us-gaap--StatementClassOfStockAxis__us-gaap--SeriesDPreferredStockMember_z2xkdlnq1PLe" title="Preferred dividends"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DividendsPreferredStock" contextRef="From2024-01-012024-12-31_us-gaap_SeriesDPreferredStockMember" id="Fact001149" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">22,500</ix:nonFraction></span>
and $<span id="xdx_90A_eus-gaap--DividendsPreferredStock_c20230101__20231231__us-gaap--StatementClassOfStockAxis__us-gaap--SeriesDPreferredStockMember_zlHlQnZRiQO6" title="Preferred dividends"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DividendsPreferredStock" contextRef="From2023-01-012023-12-31_us-gaap_SeriesDPreferredStockMember" id="Fact001151" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">30,000</ix:nonFraction></span>
in preferred dividends, respectively. At December 31, 2024 and 2023, declared but unpaid preferred dividends totaled $<span id="xdx_906_eus-gaap--DividendsPreferredStockPaidinkind_c20240101__20241231__us-gaap--StatementClassOfStockAxis__us-gaap--SeriesDPreferredStockMember_zI7aGkHBsubf" title="Preferred dividends"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DividendsPreferredStockPaidinkind" contextRef="From2024-01-012024-12-31_us-gaap_SeriesDPreferredStockMember" id="Fact001153" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">52,500</ix:nonFraction></span> and $<span id="xdx_90A_eus-gaap--DividendsPreferredStockPaidinkind_c20230101__20231231__us-gaap--StatementClassOfStockAxis__us-gaap--SeriesDPreferredStockMember_zIjvMCnKXc4b" title="Preferred dividends"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:DividendsPreferredStockPaidinkind" contextRef="From2023-01-012023-12-31_us-gaap_SeriesDPreferredStockMember" id="Fact001155" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">30,000</ix:nonFraction></span>, respectively,

December 31, 2024 and 2023, the Company had <span id="xdx_906_eus-gaap--ClassOfWarrantOrRightOutstanding_iI_pid_c20241231__us-gaap--StatementEquityComponentsAxis__us-gaap--WarrantMember_zHVb11Shm6Ah" title="Warrants to purchase common stock"><span id="xdx_90A_eus-gaap--ClassOfWarrantOrRightOutstanding_iI_pid_c20231231__us-gaap--StatementEquityComponentsAxis__us-gaap--WarrantMember_zUsiAzC6zi28" title="Warrants to purchase common stock"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightOutstanding" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_us-gaap_WarrantMember" id="Fact001173" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" unitRef="Shares"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightOutstanding" contextRef="AsOf2023-12-31_us-gaap_WarrantMember" id="Fact001175" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" unitRef="Shares">177,500</ix:nonFraction></ix:nonFraction></span></span>
of outstanding warrants to purchase common stock at a weighted average exercise price of $<span id="xdx_90F_eus-gaap--ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1_iI_c20241231__us-gaap--StatementEquityComponentsAxis__us-gaap--WarrantMember_z8E41NmfjD68" title="Weighted average exercise price"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ClassOfWarrantOrRightExercisePriceOfWarrantsOrRights1" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_us-gaap_WarrantMember" id="Fact001177" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" unitRef="USDPShares">2.25</ix:nonFraction></span>

warrants outstanding at December 31, 2024 was <span id="xdx_903_eus-gaap--ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardEquityInstrumentsOtherThanOptionsOutstandingWeightedAverageRemainingContractualTerms_dtY_c20240101__20241231__us-gaap--StatementEquityComponentsAxis__us-gaap--WarrantMember_zq1LYym00dnj" title="Common stock warrants term"><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-012024-12-31_us-gaap_WarrantMember" format="ixt-sec:duryear" id="Fact001181" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardEquityInstrumentsOtherThanOptionsOutstandingWeightedAverageRemainingContractualTerms">1.0</ix:nonNumeric></span>

</ix:nonNumeric><p id="xdx_8AD_zVkrpYPs7MNi" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><b>Common
Stock Options</b></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">On
January 6, 2022, the Company issued a three-year option to purchase <span id="xdx_902_eus-gaap--ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardSharesIssuedInPeriod_c20220106__20220106_zI9Za4tNltmg" title="Shares issued option to purchase shares of common stock"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardSharesIssuedInPeriod" contextRef="From2022-01-062022-01-06" id="Fact001237" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" unitRef="Shares">30,000</ix:nonFraction></span>
shares of our common stock at $<span id="xdx_908_eus-gaap--ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionsOutstandingWeightedAverageExercisePrice_iI_c20220106_zUDM9h8hp8Z2" title="Weighted average exercise price"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionsOutstandingWeightedAverageExercisePrice" contextRef="AsOf2022-01-06" id="Fact001239" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" unitRef="USDPShares">6.00</ix:nonFraction></span>
per share. As of December 31, 2024 and 2023, the Company had a total of <span id="xdx_904_eus-gaap--ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardSharesIssuedInPeriod_c20240101__20241231_zMAC4C1VwO4e" title="Shares issued option to purchase shares of common stock"><span id="xdx_906_eus-gaap--ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardSharesIssuedInPeriod_c20230101__20231231_zApDJewZjMHa" title="Shares issued option to purchase shares of common stock"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardSharesIssuedInPeriod" contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" id="Fact001241" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" unitRef="Shares"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardSharesIssuedInPeriod" contextRef="From2023-01-012023-12-31" id="Fact001243" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="INF" unitRef="Shares">30,000</ix:nonFraction></ix:nonFraction></span></span>
outstanding options to purchase common stock. The aggregate intrinsic value of common stock options outstanding as of December 31,
2024 was $<span id="xdx_90A_eus-gaap--ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionsOutstandingIntrinsicValue_iI_c20241231_zgT1RIiuq7Xg" title="Aggregate intrinsic value"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardOptionsOutstandingIntrinsicValue" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31" id="Fact001245" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">0</ix:nonFraction></span>.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

</ix:nonNumeric><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" escape="true" id="Fact001297" name="GBCS:SaleOfGoodwillDisclosureTextBlock"><p id="xdx_80F_ecustom--SaleOfGoodwillDisclosureTextBlock_zpJ4mUb0Plcc" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><b>5.&#160;<span id="xdx_82D_zA3rqkMf6kGh">SALE
OF GOODWILL HUNTING</span></b></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">The Company&#8217;s wholly-owned subsidiary, Goodwill
Hunting, LLC (the &#8220;Seller&#8221;),  consummated and closed the sale of property located in Macon, Bibb County, Georgia, including
the skilled nursing facility known as Archway Transitional Care Center1 (collectively, &#8220;the Archway Property&#8221;). In accordance
with the original Purchase and Sale Agreement (the &#8220;PSA&#8221;) executed on May 1, 2024, Bibb County Holdings II, LLC (the &#8220;Purchaser&#8221;)
has purchased the Archway Property for $<span id="xdx_90D_eus-gaap--PaymentsToAcquireLandHeldForUse_pn4n6_c20240501__20240501__dei--LegalEntityAxis__custom--GoodwillHuntingLLCMember_zha3ecEaAFc" title="Property purchased"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:PaymentsToAcquireLandHeldForUse" contextRef="From2024-05-012024-05-01_custom_GoodwillHuntingLLCMember" id="Fact001299" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-4" scale="6" unitRef="USD">6.75</ix:nonFraction></span>&#160;million. The sale was completed on June 18, 2024.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" escape="true" id="Fact001301" name="GBCS:ScheduleOfSaleTableTextBlock"><p id="xdx_89F_ecustom--ScheduleOfSaleTableTextBlock_zgjNBFwerWY3" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">A summary

</ix:nonNumeric><p id="xdx_8AC_znqD6paeG7ti" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0"><b>&#160;</b></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0; text-align: justify">Lessees were responsible for payment of insurance, taxes, and other charges
while under the lease. Should the lessees not pay all such charges as required under the leases, or if there is no tenant, the Company
may become liable for such operating expenses. We have been required to cover those expenses at Glen Eagle as well as the Southern Hills
SNF, ALF and ILF, Meadowview, Higher Call, Edwards, Fairland, Sparta, and Warrenton properties.</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">The Company
and its subsidiaries are subject to income taxes on income arising in, or derived from, the tax jurisdictions in which they operate.
The Company files federal, Alabama, Arkansas, Colorado, Georgia, and Oklahoma income tax returns. The Company is current with all its
federal and state tax filings. The 2020 through 2023 tax years generally remain subject to examination by the IRS and various state taxing
authorities, although the Company is not currently under examination in any jurisdiction.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: center"><span style="font-size: 10pt"></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: center"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">Deferred
income taxes reflect the net tax effects of temporary differences between the carrying amount of assets and liabilities for financial
reporting purposes and the amounts used for income tax purposes.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

</ix:nonNumeric><p id="xdx_8AE_z8HEzbJ6FrWe" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>




<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">The valuation
allowance at December 31, 2024 and 2023 was primarily related to federal net operating loss carryforwards that, in the judgment of management,
are not more-likely-than-not to be realized. In assessing the realizability of deferred tax assets, management considers whether it is
more-likely-than-not that some portion or all of the deferred tax assets will not be realized. The ultimate realization of deferred tax
assets is dependent upon the generation of future taxable income during the periods in which those temporary differences become deductible.
Management considers the scheduled reversal of deferred tax liabilities (including the impact of available carryback and carryforward
periods), projected future taxable income, and tax-planning strategies in making this assessment. Based upon the level of historical
taxable income and projections for future taxable income over the periods in which the deferred tax assets are deductible, management
believes it is more-likely-than-not that the Company will not realize the benefits of these deductible differences, net of the existing
valuation allowance at December 31, 2024.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">As of December
31, 2024 and 2023, the Company has $<span id="xdx_90F_eus-gaap--OperatingLossCarryforwards_iI_c20241231__us-gaap--IncomeTaxAuthorityAxis__us-gaap--DomesticCountryMember_zCxdPgpeLah3"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:OperatingLossCarryforwards" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_us-gaap_DomesticCountryMember" id="Fact001418" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">11,028,602</ix:nonFraction></span></span>
<span style="font-size: 10pt">and $<span id="xdx_90A_eus-gaap--OperatingLossCarryforwards_iI_c20231231__us-gaap--IncomeTaxAuthorityAxis__us-gaap--DomesticCountryMember_zKL9N4zZ4QS9"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:OperatingLossCarryforwards" contextRef="AsOf2023-12-31_us-gaap_DomesticCountryMember" id="Fact001419" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">10,208,276</ix:nonFraction>
</span></span><span style="font-size: 10pt">federal net operating losses, among which, if not used, $<span id="xdx_904_ecustom--OperatingNotLossCarryforwards_iI_c20241231__us-gaap--IncomeTaxAuthorityAxis__us-gaap--DomesticCountryMember_zYuuDBG1mWxl"><ix:nonFraction name="GBCS:OperatingNotLossCarryforwards" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_us-gaap_DomesticCountryMember" id="Fact001420" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">630,913</ix:nonFraction></span></span>
<span style="font-size: 10pt">and $<span id="xdx_90D_ecustom--OperatingNotLossCarryforwards_iI_c20231231__us-gaap--IncomeTaxAuthorityAxis__us-gaap--DomesticCountryMember_zk9vPrducXvi"><ix:nonFraction name="GBCS:OperatingNotLossCarryforwards" contextRef="AsOf2023-12-31_us-gaap_DomesticCountryMember" id="Fact001421" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">630,913</ix:nonFraction></span></span>
<span style="font-size: 10pt">would begin to expire starting <span id="xdx_902_ecustom--OperatingLossCarryforwardsExpirationYear_c20240101__20241231_zLVR0HnpMUol"><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" id="Fact001422" name="GBCS:OperatingLossCarryforwardsExpirationYear">2037</ix:nonNumeric></span></span><span style="font-size: 10pt">.
The remaining $<span id="xdx_904_ecustom--OperatingLossRemaining_iI_c20241231__us-gaap--IncomeTaxAuthorityAxis__us-gaap--DomesticCountryMember_z6fP70mBuYxf"><ix:nonFraction name="GBCS:OperatingLossRemaining" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_us-gaap_DomesticCountryMember" id="Fact001423" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">10,397,689</ix:nonFraction></span></span>
<span style="font-size: 10pt">net operating losses are <span id="xdx_903_eus-gaap--OperatingLossCarryforwardsLimitationsOnUse_c20240101__20241231_zy7lE45rKtd7"><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" id="Fact001424" name="us-gaap:OperatingLossCarryforwardsLimitationsOnUse">subject
to the 80% taxable income limitation.</ix:nonNumeric></span></span> <span style="font-size: 10pt">As of December 31, 2024 and 2023, the Company has $<span id="xdx_908_eus-gaap--OperatingLossCarryforwards_iI_c20241231__us-gaap--IncomeTaxAuthorityAxis__us-gaap--StateAndLocalJurisdictionMember_zGcrWMiXjT9e"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:OperatingLossCarryforwards" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_us-gaap_StateAndLocalJurisdictionMember" id="Fact001425" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">17,068,175</ix:nonFraction></span></span>
<span style="font-size: 10pt">and $<span id="xdx_909_eus-gaap--OperatingLossCarryforwards_iI_c20231231__us-gaap--IncomeTaxAuthorityAxis__us-gaap--StateAndLocalJurisdictionMember_zfqVFwwTqQq3"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:OperatingLossCarryforwards" contextRef="AsOf2023-12-31_us-gaap_StateAndLocalJurisdictionMember" id="Fact001426" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">15,670,635</ix:nonFraction></span></span>
<span style="font-size: 10pt">state net operating losses.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">When more
than a 50% change in ownership occurs, over a three-year period, as defined, the Tax Reform Act of 1986 limits the utilization of net
operating loss carry forwards in the years following the change in ownership. In September 2013, the Company had a split-off, in which
the business activities changed from gaming casinos into real estate related to long-term care. The Company determined that the pre-2013
net operating losses of $<span id="xdx_90C_eus-gaap--OperatingLossCarryforwards_iI_c20241231__us-gaap--TaxPeriodAxis__us-gaap--TaxYear2013Member_zQBvz3CAv6M" title="Operating loss carryforwards"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:OperatingLossCarryforwards" contextRef="AsOf2024-12-31_us-gaap_TaxYear2013Member" id="Fact001428" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">4,047,175</ix:nonFraction></span> were subject to the limitation set forth under Internal Revenue Code Section 382 and thus wrote off
such net operating losses in 2022. No determination has been made regarding whether another ownership change had occurred during 2014
and 2023.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">We have
evaluated whether there were material uncertain tax positions requiring recognition in our financial statements. During the period of
2016 to 2021, the Company had recorded bad debt allowances that should have had been adjusted on income tax returns but were not. Consent
from the IRS is needed to change the accounting method on tax treatment of bad debt allowance. As of December 31, 2021, the Company has
$<span id="xdx_90D_ecustom--BadDebtAllowance_c20210101__20211231_zNwUxLJCXz4i" title="Bad debt allowance"><ix:nonFraction name="GBCS:BadDebtAllowance" contextRef="From2021-01-012021-12-31" id="Fact001430" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">1,901,203</ix:nonFraction></span> bad debt allowance. The Company recognized $<span id="xdx_903_eus-gaap--UnrecognizedTaxBenefits_iI_dxL_c20211231_zT59IfJjInEf" title="Unrecognized tax benefits::XDX::-"><span style="-sec-ix-hidden: xdx2ixbrl1432">0</span></span> uncertain tax liability in the calculation of deferred tax assets from NOL.
When filing its 2022 income tax return, the Company submitted an application to change the accounting method on bad debt allowance. <span style="background-color: white">The
company policy is to treat tax-related interest and penalties as income tax expense.</span> As of December 31, 2024, the Company did
not identify any other uncertain tax position.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

    <td style="border-bottom: Black 1pt solid; text-align: left"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></td><td style="border-bottom: Black 1pt solid; text-align: right"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><span style="-sec-ix-hidden: xdx2ixbrl1445">-</span></span></td><td style="padding-bottom: 1pt; text-align: left"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></td><td style="padding-bottom: 1pt"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></td>

  </table>

</ix:nonNumeric><p id="xdx_8A8_znG6tpC4Lnx2" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

</ix:nonNumeric><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" escape="true" id="Fact001448" name="us-gaap:GoodwillDisclosureTextBlock"><p id="xdx_80E_eus-gaap--GoodwillDisclosureTextBlock_zNE6BvgNgeY3" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><b>8. <span id="xdx_823_zlP23TM9Qgu">GOODWILL</span></b></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">Goodwill
is tested for impairment at a reporting unit level on an annual basis or when an event occurs, or circumstances change that would more
likely than not reduce the fair value of a reporting unit below its carrying amount. During the years ended December 31, 2024 and 2023,
the Company recorded <span id="xdx_904_eus-gaap--GoodwillImpairmentLoss_do_c20240101__20241231_zB6ujkA41Mbb" title="Goodwill impairment loss"><span id="xdx_900_eus-gaap--GoodwillImpairmentLoss_do_c20230101__20231231_zPoETbjnnUK5" title="Goodwill impairment loss"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:GoodwillImpairmentLoss" contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" id="Fact001450" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" decimals="0" unitRef="USD"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:GoodwillImpairmentLoss" contextRef="From2023-01-012023-12-31" id="Fact001452" format="ixt-sec:numwordsen" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">no</ix:nonFraction></ix:nonFraction></span></span> impairment of Goodwill.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" escape="true" id="Fact001454" name="us-gaap:ScheduleOfGoodwillTextBlock"><p id="xdx_89A_eus-gaap--ScheduleOfGoodwillTextBlock_znfDwbPJopN2" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">Following
is a summary of goodwill for the years ended December 31, 2024 and 2023:</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

</ix:nonNumeric><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" escape="true" id="Fact001466" name="us-gaap:LegalMattersAndContingenciesTextBlock"><p id="xdx_80F_eus-gaap--LegalMattersAndContingenciesTextBlock_zISHlf7AU7xl" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><b>9. <span id="xdx_82A_zfsSSKNZxBsg">LEGAL PROCEEDINGS</span></b></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">The Company
and/or its affiliated subsidiaries provide patient care at or through their facilities. As such, the Company and its affiliated subsidiaries
are subject from time to time to claims of negligence resulting in injury or death to residents. The Company maintains comprehensive
general liability insurance and professional liability insurance in sufficient amounts to cover most material exposure resulting from
these claims. The cost of defense is generally covered by these liability policies subject to reasonable reserves and deductibles. Nevertheless
the Company does have exposure to these claims which in some cases can be material. There can be no assurance that the Company&#8217;s
portfolio of insurance products will be adequate to cover all potential exposure or prevent material adverse financial losses.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">The following represent some
of the matters pending as of the date of this Report:</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><i>Hines
v. Global Abbeville LLC, d/b/a Glen Eagle, et al, Superior Court of Warren County, State of Georgia, Civil Action No.2023-CV-094</i></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><i>&#160;</i></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">This is
a personal injury lawsuit filed on September 11, 2023 against various defendants arising from injuries several months after being admitted
to the Glen Eagle facility. The complaint alleges that the facility was negligent in the care administered to the plaintiff which resulted
in the injuries, which the Company denies. The Company has referred the litigation to its insurance company for management and believes
that its exposure in this matter is de minimus.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><i>Hunter
v. Global Abbeville LLC, d/b/a Glen Eagle, et al, State Court of Fulton County, State of Georgia</i></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">This is a wrongful death lawsuit
filed on June 20, 2023 against various defendants alleging negligence that led to the death of the plaintiff&#8217;s mother who had been
admitted to the facility. The complaint alleges that the Company deviated from the standard of care that caused injuries that ultimately
led to the death of the patient, which the Company denies. The Company has referred the litigation to its insurance company for management
and believes that its exposure in this matter is de minimus.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

</ix:nonNumeric><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" escape="true" id="Fact001468" name="us-gaap:CommitmentsAndContingenciesDisclosureTextBlock"><p id="xdx_808_eus-gaap--CommitmentsAndContingenciesDisclosureTextBlock_zwWkheQmTgO4" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><b>10.
<span id="xdx_82B_zsROFoLnupk4">COMMITMENTS AND CONTINGENCIES</span></b></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><i>General
and Professional Liability Insurance and Lawsuits</i></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">The senior
care industry has experienced significant increases in both the number of personal injury/wrongful death claims and in the severity of
awards based upon alleged negligence by skilled nursing facilities and their employees in providing care to residents. The Company has
been, and continues to be, subject to claims and legal actions that arise in the ordinary course of business, including potential claims
related to patient care and treatment. The defense of these lawsuits&#160;<i>may&#160;</i>result in significant legal costs, regardless
of the outcome, and can result in large settlement amounts or damage awards. The Company purchases insurance through third party providers
that provides coverage for these claims.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">There is
certain additional litigation incidental to our business, none of which, based upon information available to date, would be material
to our financial position, results of operations, or cash flows. In addition, the long&#8211;term care industry is continuously subject
to scrutiny by governmental regulators, which could result in litigation or claims related to regulatory compliance matters.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><i>Governmental
Regulations</i></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">Laws and
regulations governing the Medicare, Medicaid and other federal healthcare programs are complex and subject to interpretation. Management
believes that it is following all applicable laws and regulations in all material respects. However, compliance with such laws and regulations
can be subject to future government review and interpretation as well as significant regulatory action including fines, penalties, and
exclusions from the Medicare, Medicaid and other federal healthcare programs.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt"></span></p>

<ix:exclude><!-- Field: Page; Sequence: 67; Value: 1 -->
    <div style="border-bottom: Black 1pt solid; margin-bottom: 6pt"><table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; font-size: 10pt"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: left"><td style="text-align: center; width: 100%">F-<!-- Field: Sequence; Type: Arabic; Name: PageNo -->21<!-- Field: /Sequence --></td></tr></table></div>
    <div style="break-before: page; margin-top: 6pt"><table cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; font-size: 10pt"><tr style="vertical-align: top; text-align: left"><td style="text-align: center; width: 100%">&#160;</td></tr></table></div>
    <!-- Field: /Page --></ix:exclude>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

</ix:nonNumeric><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="From2024-01-01to2024-12-31" escape="true" id="Fact001470" name="us-gaap:SubsequentEventsTextBlock"><p id="xdx_809_eus-gaap--SubsequentEventsTextBlock_zqOaqQkLU5z9" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><b>11. <span id="xdx_820_z2S3sYC4IM75">SUBSEQUENT
EVENTS</span></b></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">Effective
on February 7, 2025, the Company caused three of the Company&#8217;s wholly-owned subsidiaries Global Abbeville Property, LLC,
Dodge NH, LLC, and ATL/WARR, LLC, each a Georgia limited liability company (each a &#8220;Seller&#8221;), to execute and deliver a definitive
Purchase and Sale Agreement (&#8220;PSA&#8221;) with Abbeville Propco Holdco, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company (&#8220;Purchaser&#8221;)
and also caused a fourth subsidiary, Providence HR, LLC, a Georgia limited liability company, to execute and deliver an additional Purchase
and Sale Agreement (also a &#8220;Seller&#8221;) with the Purchaser. Pursuant to both PSAs each Seller agreed to sell substantially all
of the real and personal property owned by each, namely the skilled nursing facilities located at (i) 206 Main Street East, Abbeville,
Georgia, 31001, upon which is located that certain 101-bed skilled nursing facility commonly known as &#8220;Glen Eagle Healthcare &amp;
Rehab&#8221; (the &#8220;Glen Eagle Facility&#8221;), (ii) 556 Chester Highway, Eastman, Georgia, 31023, upon which is located that certain
100-bed skilled nursing facility commonly known as &#8220;Eastman Healthcare &amp; Rehab&#8221; (the &#8220;Eastman Facility&#8221;),
(iii) 60 Providence Street, Sparta, Georgia, 31087, upon which is located that certain 71-bed skilled nursing facility commonly known
as &#8220;Providence of Sparta Health and Rehabilitation&#8221; (the &#8220;Sparta Facility&#8221;), and (iv) 813 Atlanta Highway, Warrenton,
Georgia, 30828, upon which is located that certain 110-bed skilled nursing facility commonly known as &#8220;Warrenton Health and Rehabilitation&#8221;
(the &#8220;Warrenton Facility&#8221; and together with the Eastman Facility, Glen Eagle Facility, and Sparta Facility, the &#8220;Facilities&#8221;).</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">The Sparta
Facility was covered under a separate PSA to reflect the Purchaser&#8217;s desire and intent to assume the HUD loan secured by the Sparta
Facility. The purchase price to be paid by Purchaser for the four (4) Facilities under the two PSAs is an aggregate of $<span id="xdx_904_eus-gaap--LineOfCredit_iI_pn5n6_c20250207__dei--LegalEntityAxis__custom--SpartaFacilityMember__us-gaap--SubsequentEventTypeAxis__us-gaap--SubsequentEventMember_zgEljjPUnHge" title="Line of credit"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:LineOfCredit" contextRef="AsOf2025-02-07_custom_SpartaFacilityMember_us-gaap_SubsequentEventMember" id="Fact001472" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5" scale="6" unitRef="USD">27.0</ix:nonFraction></span> million,
subject to certain prorations, holdbacks and adjustments customary in transactions of this nature. Consummation of the PSAs are contingent
upon numerous conditions, including, without limitation, satisfactory completion of due diligence and other conditions customary in transaction
of this nature. There can be no assurance that the PSAs will be consummated.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><i>Operations
Transfer Agreements</i></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><i>&#160;</i></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">The Facilities
are operated by separate, wholly-owned subsidiaries of the Company, namely Global Abbeville, LLC, a Georgia limited liability company,
Global Eastman, LLC, a Georgia limited liability company, Selectis Sparta, LLC, a Georgia limited liability company, and Selectis Warrenton,
LLC, a Georgia limited liability company (collectively, the &#8220;Existing Operators&#8221;). Concurrently with the execution of the
PSA, the Company caused three of the Existing Operators to execute Operations Transfer Agreement (&#8220;OTA&#8221;) with a new entity
affiliated with the Purchaser, Abbeville Opco Holdco LLC, a Delaware limited liability company (the &#8220;Abbeville New Operator&#8221;)
and also cause the Existing Operator Selectis Sparta, LLC to execute an OTA with Sparta Opco Holdcso LLC, a Delaware limited liability
company (the &#8220;Sparta New Operator&#8221;). If consummated, of which there can be no assurance, the OTA will govern the transfer
of the skilled nursing operations from the Existing Operators to the New Operators. Consummation of the OTA&#8217;s is contingent upon
the consummation of the PSAs as well as other conditions customary in transaction of this nature.</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt"><i>Escrow
Agreement</i></span></p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify">&#160;</p>

<p style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: justify"><span style="font-size: 10pt">In connection
with the PSA, the Company&#8217;s three wholly-owned subsidiaries Global Abbeville Property, LLC, Dodge NH, LLC, and ATL/WARR, LLC, each
a Georgia limited liability company (each a &#8220;Seller&#8221;), Abbeville Propco Holdco LLC, a Delaware limited liability company
(individually and collectively, &#8220;Purchaser&#8221;), and Landmark Abstract Agency, LLC (the &#8220;Escrow Agent&#8221;), entered
into an Escrow Agreement dated February 7, 2025 whereby the Purchaser delivered or will deliver to Escrow Agent an initial deposit of
Five Hundred Thousand and No/100 Dollars ($<span id="xdx_90B_eus-gaap--EscrowDeposit_iI_c20250207__us-gaap--TypeOfArrangementAxis__custom--EscrowAgreementMember_zTYessfVZ4sa"><ix:nonFraction name="us-gaap:EscrowDeposit" contextRef="AsOf2025-02-07_custom_EscrowAgreementMember" id="Fact001473" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="0" unitRef="USD">500,000</ix:nonFraction></span></span><span style="font-size: 10pt">)
(the &#8220;Initial Deposit&#8221;). The Initial Deposit and any other sums deposited in escrow by Purchaser under the PSA, will be held
by the Escrow Agent, together with any interest and dividends earned thereon, if any (the &#8220;Escrow Fund&#8221;).</span></p>

</ix:nonNumeric><p id="xdx_819_z2mKVtGrCEDb" style="font: 10pt Times New Roman, Times, Serif; margin: 0pt 0pt 0pt 0; text-align: center"><span style="font-size: 10pt">&#160;</span></p>
